div.head: 490. Telegram From the Embassy in Jordan to the Department of StateSource: Department of State, Central Files, 684A.85/7–2356. Confidential. Received at 3:06 p.m. Repeated to Ankara, Baghdad, Beirut, Cairo, Damascus, London, and Paris.
div.p:
57. Hammarskjold spent two hours Amman morning July 21,Hammarskjöld stopped in Amman the morning of July 21 for talks with Jordanian officials before continuing to Cairo that day. conferring with Foreign Minister,Awni Abdel Hadi. Under Secretary Foreign AffairsIbrahim Hashim. General Ali Abu Nuwar and Ambassador Abdul Munim Rifai.Jordanian Ambassador to the United States. Under Secretary Foreign Affairs says Hammarskjold told them he was concerned situation Jordan-Israeli border following Burns talks Ben GurionSee Documents 417 and 418. and King Hussein,See Document 421. particularly alleged or real troop concentrations. Because UNSYG felt little progress without first hand contact he had come from Geneva at this time. Said he had informed Ben Gurion (A) Israeli retaliation Arab raids or infiltration did not constitute defense and would adversely effect Israeli position world opinion and (B) this also applied retaliatory acts Jordan which
should increase efforts keep frontier quiet and refrain bellicose acts or words.

In reply HKJ told UNSYG Jordan doing utmost combat infiltration. Not only has Jordan law against infiltration but military has issued orders to shoot all infiltrators regardless direction they going. Hammarskjold assured Jordan has no aggressive intentions. On other hand country cannot allow itself be taken by surprise. Therefore, when in best estimate Jordan country is threatened HKJ considers itself free take steps necessary adequate self-defense.
Press reports UNSYG said visit continuation last trip area and he returned Amman because tense conditions Israeli border.
Hammarskjold departed for Cairo about noon July 21.
